data.author: Charles Dickens
data.content: Subdue your appetites, my dears, and you've conquered human nature.

data.author: Charles Dickens
data.content: No one is useless in this world who lightens the burdens of another.

data.author: Thomas Edison
data.content: Opportunity is missed by most people because it is dressed in overalls and looks like work.

data.author: Thomas Edison
data.content: Hell, there are no rules here-- we're trying to accomplish something.

data.author: Thomas Edison
data.content: If we all did the things we are capable of doing, we would literally astound ourselves.

data.author: Thomas Edison
data.content: Genius is one per cent inspiration, ninety-nine per cent perspiration.

data.author: Charles Dickens
data.content: Train up a fig tree in the way it should go, and when you are old sit under the shade of it.

data.author: Charles Dickens
data.content: I do not know the American gentleman, god forgive me for putting two such words together.

data.author: Thomas Edison
data.content: To invent, you need a good imagination and a pile of junk.

data.author: Thomas Edison
data.content: If I find 10,000 ways something won't work, I haven't failed. I am not discouraged, because every wrong attempt discarded is another step forward.
